chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI CP No.S-737 of 2025 (Mr. M. Rizwan Yasin v. Dr. Faiza Mushtaque and others) ____________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For orders on office objection as at A 2. For orders on CMA No.5882/2025 3. For hearing of main case 15.12.2025 Mr. Fayyaz Aslam, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Ghulam Mujtaba Phull, advocate for the respondent --------- Nisar Ahmed Bhanbhro, J. Learned counsel for the petitioner files statement dated 15.12.2025, which is taken on record. Through this petition, the petitioner has called in question the order dated 12.08.2025 passed by the learned Additional District Judge-VI, Malir, Karachi, in Family Appeal No.94/2024 wherein the order dated 16.11.2024 passed by the learned XIth Family judge, Karachi-Malir, whereby application for implementation of the order dated 19.02.2024 passed by this Court in CP No.970 of 2023 was declined. 2. Perusal of the order dated 19.02.2024 reflects that this court in the said proceedings directed the petitioner to pay maintenance and the respondent was directed to adhere the provisions of settlement agreement embodied in the judgment dated 23.10.2021 and to provide access to the father through internet/WhatsApp for meeting with minor which should be atleast for one hour a week. 3. Learned counsel for the petitioner has failed to point out that such access has been denied to the father. Therefore, the present petition, being devoid of merit, is dismissed along with the pending application(s). So far as the implementation of the order dated 19.02.2024 regarding visitation rights of the father with minor as settled between the parties is concerned, the petitioner may approach the concerned trial Court for enforcement of those rights.

Therefore, the present petition, being devoid of merit, is dismissed along with the pending application(s). So far as the implementation of the order dated 19.02.2024 regarding visitation rights of the father with minor as settled between the parties is concerned, the petitioner may approach the concerned trial Court for enforcement of those rights. JUDGE Nadir*
